figures.caption: Figure 5: Results on a fully occluded scene from YCBMultiTrack-Real-World. After the object becomes completely occluded and later re-enters the scene, our method successfully recovers the object pose, whereas BundleSDF [30] loses track and preserves a stale object orientation.
Table 2: The results in the YCBInEOATdataset [29]. Comparison of BundleSDF [30] and Ours by sequence group on ADD-S/ADD AUC. We refer the reader to the Appendix for the full sequence name.

Figure 4: Comparison between low-texture and textured scenes. The green bounding box shows the pose estimated by the proposed method, while the red box denotes the ground truth. Point colors indicate the tracker’s uncertainty, with lighter colors corresponding to higher uncertainty and darker colors to more confident tracks; we visualize only points deemed visible by the tracker. In the AP12 sequence, where our method attains a lower ADD score, many tracked points lie on largely featureless regions and exhibit high uncertainty. We additionally observe that these points fluctuate across frames, which leads to unstable correspondences and degrades pose estimation accuracy. In contrast, AP14 provides richer texture, enabling more stable point tracks and more accurate pose estimates. A similar trend appears in the Bleach sequence: v1 includes an extended interval dominated by a nearly uniform white side view, which yields unstable point tracks and consequently noisier pose estimates.

Figure 2: Overview of the Point2Pose framework. A 2D point tracker propagates object keypoints from RGB images and segmentation masks for frame-to-map registration and pose-hypothesis selection. In parallel, segmented depth is fused into an object-centric TSDF for dense reconstruction, while graph optimization maintains global consistency and supports robust tracking under occlusion.

Table 3: Comparison of BundleSDF and Ours on ADD-S and ADD, grouped by object count. Left: YCBMultiTrack-Synthetic. Right: YCBMultiTrack-Real-World. We refer the reader to the Appendix for the full object name. †BundleSDF averages exclude the failed sequence group.
